title: Holy Monday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## Sermon: "Holy Monday" | March 26, 2018 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

March 26, 2018 was Monday of Holy Week, the final week of Lent leading to Easter (April 1, 2018). The Revised Common Lectionary for this day would have included passages reflecting on Christ's passion and the approaching crucifixion, likely drawing from Isaiah's suffering servant passages and Gospel accounts of Jesus' final days. The sermon title "Holy Monday" directly references this pivotal moment in the Christian calendar when many parishes shift into intensive Holy Week observances and practices.

---

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was still navigating internal tensions regarding LGBTQ+ inclusion and marriage equality in the wake of General Convention 2015 and 2018 preparations. The Diocese of Colorado was preparing for its annual Diocesan Convention (typically held in fall), and the broader Anglican Communion remained fragmented over sexuality and biblical interpretation. No major Episcopal leadership changes or crises were dominating news in late March 2018, though ongoing debates about marriage and ordination continued to shape parish conversations.

---

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in early spring with pleasant weather after a mild winter. Local news focused on state legislative sessions (Colorado's legislative session runs January-May), with education funding and school safety being prominent topics following national school shooting concerns. Downtown Denver and the Capitol Hill neighborhood, where Saint John's Cathedral is located, were experiencing continued gentrification and development pressures that characterized the metro area in the 2010s.

---

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Trump administration was in turmoil over the firing of Secretary of State Rex Tillerson (March 13, replacing him with CIA director Mike Pompeo), dominating political coverage. The Cambridge Analytica scandal was intensifying, with major revelations about Facebook data misuse and the company's role in political campaigns emerging around this exact time. Former Trump campaign chief Paul Manafort was facing serious legal charges and was in active litigation, and the Mueller investigation continued to dominate news cycles with new developments.

---

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The poisoning of Russian former spy Sergei Skripal and his daughter in Salisbury, England (March 4) was still developing, with Britain and Russia in an escalating diplomatic crisis over the attack. The Syrian civil war continued with ongoing humanitarian crises and Russian military involvement. The Winter Olympics had concluded just weeks earlier (February 2018), and international attention was shifting to spring political developments globally.

---

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a period of cultural reckoning around sexual harassment and power dynamics following the #MeToo movement's emergence in late 2017, which continued to surface new allegations and institutional reforms. March Madness (NCAA basketball tournament) was underway, capturing significant national attention. The cultural moment was one of deep political polarization, with many Americans experiencing anxiety about institutional trust, national leadership, and social cohesion. A Colorado congregation would have been aware of both national tensions and local growth pressures reshaping their city's character.
